args.name: -l
args.description: to clone from a local repository

args.name: --no-hardlinks
args.description: don't use local hardlinks, always copy

args.name: --[no-]jobs
args.description: number of submodules cloned in parallel

args.name: --[no-]depth
args.description: create a shallow clone of that depth

args.name: --[no-]single-branch
args.description: clone only one branch, HEAD or --branch

args.name: --[no-]tags
args.description: clone tags, and make later fetches not to follow them

args.name: --[no-]sparse
args.description: initialize sparse-checkout file to include only files at root

args.name: -A
args.description: add changes from all tracked and untracked files

args.name: --[no-]refresh
args.description: don't add, only refresh the index

args.name: --[no-]ignore-errors
args.description: just skip files which cannot be added because of errors

args.name: --[no-]sparse
args.description: allow updating entries outside of the sparse-checkout cone

args.name: --[no-]chmod
args.description: override the executable bit of the listed files

args.name: -f
args.description: force move/rename even if target exists

args.name: --[no-]sparse
args.description: allow updating entries outside of the sparse-checkout cone

args.name: -m
args.description: perform a 3-way merge with the new branch
checkout our version for unmerged files
checkout their version for unmerged files

args.name: --[no-]sparse
args.description: allow updating entries outside of the sparse-checkout cone

args.name: -z
args.description: output diff-raw with lines terminated with NUL.

args.name: --numstat
args.description: show numeric diffstat instead of patch.

args.name: --name-only
args.description: show only names of changed files.

args.name: --full-index
args.description: show full object name on index lines.

args.name: --abbrev=<n>
args.description: abbreviate object names in diff-tree header and diff-raw.

args.name: -l<n>
args.description: limit rename attempts up to <n> paths.

args.name: -S<string>
args.description: find filepair whose only one side contains the string.

args.name: --[no-]cached
args.description: search in index instead of in the work tree

args.name: --no-index
args.description: find in contents not managed by git

args.name: --[no-]untracked
args.description: search in both tracked and untracked files

args.name: -I
args.description: don't match patterns in binary files

args.name: --[no-]textconv
args.description: process binary files with textconv filters

args.name: --[no-]column
args.description: show column number of first match

args.name: --[no-]full-name
args.description: show filenames relative to top directory

args.name: -c
args.description: show the number of matches instead of matching lines

args.name: --[no-]break
args.description: print empty line between matches from different files

args.name: --[no-]heading
args.description: show filename only once above matches from same file

args.name: -q
args.description: indicate hit with exit status without output

args.name: --[no-]all-match
args.description: show only matches from files that match all patterns

args.name: --[no-]ext-grep
args.description: allow calling of grep(1) (ignored by this build)

args.name: -L
args.description: trace the evolution of line range <start>,<end> or function :<funcname> in <file>

args.name: -L
args.description: trace the evolution of line range <start>,<end> or function :<funcname> in <file>

args.name: --min-batch-size
args.description: Minimum number of objects to request at a time

args.name: --[no-]sparse
args.description: Restrict the missing objects to the current sparse-checkout

args.name: -v
args.description: show hash and subject, give twice for upstream branch

args.name: --contains
args.description: print only branches that contain the commit

args.name: --[no-]abbrev[=<n>]
args.description: use <n> digits to display object names

args.name: -a
args.description: list both remote-tracking and local branches

args.name: -m
args.description: move/rename a branch and its reflog

args.name: -M
args.description: move/rename a branch, even if target exists

args.name: --[no-]omit-empty
args.description: do not output a newline after empty formatted refs

args.name: -c
args.description: copy a branch and its reflog

args.name: -C
args.description: copy a branch, even if target exists

args.name: --merged
args.description: print only branches that are merged

args.name: --no-merged
args.description: print only branches that are not merged

args.name: -v
args.description: show diff in commit message template

args.name: --[no-]reset-author
args.description: the commit is authored by me now (used with -C/-c/--amend)

args.name: --[no-]status
args.description: include status in commit message template

args.name: -i
args.description: add specified files to index for commit

args.name: -n
args.description: do not show a diffstat at the end of the merge

args.name: --[no-]stat
args.description: show a diffstat at the end of the merge

args.name: --[no-]log[=<n>]
args.description: add (at most <n>) entries from shortlog to merge commit message

args.name: --[no-]squash
args.description: create a single commit instead of doing a merge

args.name: --[no-]commit
args.description: perform a commit if the merge succeeds (default)

args.name: --ff-only
args.description: abort if fast-forward is not possible

args.name: --[no-]quit
args.description: --abort but leave index and working tree alone

args.name: --[no-]autostash
args.description: automatically stash/stash pop before and after

args.name: --[no-]keep-base
args.description: use the merge-base of upstream and branch as the current base

args.name: -v
args.description: display a diffstat of what changed upstream

args.name: -n
args.description: do not show diffstat of what changed upstream

args.name: --[no-]signoff
args.description: add a Signed-off-by trailer to each commit

args.name: --no-ff
args.description: cherry-pick all commits, even if unchanged

args.name: --abort
args.description: abort and check out the original branch

args.name: --quit
args.description: abort but keep HEAD where it is

args.name: --edit-todo
args.description: edit the todo list during an interactive rebase

args.name: --show-current-patch
args.description: show the patch file being applied or merged

args.name: -i
args.description: let the user edit the list of commits to rebase

args.name: --[no-]autosquash
args.description: move commits that begin with squash!/fixup! under -i

args.name: --[no-]update-refs
args.description: update branches that point to commits that are being rebased

args.name: --[no-]autostash
args.description: automatically stash/stash pop before and after

args.name: --[no-]root
args.description: rebase all reachable commits up to the root(s)

args.name: --no-refresh
args.description: skip refreshing the index after reset

args.name: --hard
args.description: reset HEAD, index and working tree

args.name: --merge
args.description: reset HEAD, index and working tree

args.name: --keep
args.description: reset HEAD but keep local changes

args.name: -m
args.description: perform a 3-way merge with the new branch

args.name: -n[<n>]
args.description: print <n> lines of each tag message

args.name: --contains
args.description: print only tags that contain the commit

args.name: --merged
args.description: print only tags that are merged

args.name: --no-merged
args.description: print only tags that are not merged

args.name: --[no-]omit-empty
args.description: do not output a newline after empty formatted refs

args.name: --[no-]atomic
args.description: use atomic transaction to update references

args.name: -t
args.description: fetch all tags and associated objects

args.name: --[no-]jobs
args.description: number of submodules fetched in parallel

args.name: --[no-]prefetch
args.description: modify the refspec to place all refs within refs/prefetch/

args.name: -p
args.description: prune remote-tracking branches no longer on remote

args.name: -n
args.description: do not show a diffstat at the end of the merge

args.description: show a diffstat at the end of the merge

args.name: --[no-]log[=<n>]
args.description: add (at most <n>) entries from shortlog to merge commit message

args.description: create a single commit instead of doing a merge

args.name: --[no-]commit
args.description: perform a commit if the merge succeeds (default)

args.name: --ff-only
args.description: abort if fast-forward is not possible

args.name: --[no-]verify

args.name: --[no-]autostash
args.description: automatically stash/stash pop before and after

args.name: -t
args.description: fetch all tags and associated objects

args.name: -p
args.description: prune remote-tracking branches no longer on remote
